question: Did the model correctly identify the coordinates of vertex A as (2, -1)?
Did the model correctly identify the coordinates of vertex B as (4, 3)?
Did the model correctly identify the coordinates of vertex C as (1, 2)?
Did the model calculate the final area of triangle ABC as 5?
Are the vertices A, B, and C in the generated image placed at the correct grid intersection points corresponding to (2, -1), (4, 3), and (1, 2)?
Is the coordinate system (x-axis, y-axis, origin) correctly depicted with proper orientation (x right, y up) and visible grid lines?
Are the vertices and any auxiliary points clearly labeled (e.g., A, B, C) in the generated image?
If auxiliary lines are generated (e.g., perpendiculars, bounding shapes), are they drawn straight and aligned with the grid lines or intersections?
Does the generated image accurately reflect the relative positions of the points (e.g., A is below the x-axis, B is the highest point)?
If the model describes a specific geometric construction (e.g., a bounding trapezoid or rectangle), does the image semantically represent this specific shape enclosing the triangle?
Did the model correctly interpret the grid scale (1 unit per square) to determine coordinates and lengths?
Did the model use a valid mathematical method to calculate the area (e.g., bounding box/complementary method, shoelace formula, or coordinate determinant)?
If the complementary/subtraction method was used, did the model correctly calculate the dimensions and areas of the auxiliary shapes (e.g., trapezoid area = 8, subtracted triangles = 1.5 each)?
Did the model correctly handle the negative y-coordinate of point A during length or area calculations (e.g., correctly determining the vertical distance involves subtraction of a negative)?
Are all intermediate arithmetic calculations (additions, subtractions, multiplications) correct and consistent with the final result?
Does the text description of the geometric construction match the visual elements shown in the generated image?
Are the specific coordinate values or segment lengths mentioned in the text (e.g., "length is 3") consistent with the visual representation in the image?
Are there any contradictions between the text and the image regarding the position of points (e.g., text says A is at (2, -1) but image shows it elsewhere)?
If the text references specific visual features (e.g., "point D", "shaded region", "vertical line"), are these features clearly present in the generated image?
